name: Krampuslauf Salzburg
description: The Krampus (German: [ˈkʁampʊs]) is a horned anthropomorphic figure who, in the Central and Eastern Alpine folkloric tradition, is said to accompany Saint Nicholas on visits to children during the night of 5 December (Krampusnacht; "Krampus Night"), immediately before the Feast of St. Nicholas on 6  The Salzburg Krampuslauf is one of the largest, held on December 5 (Krampusnacht), with hundreds in elaborate Krampus costumes.

name: Krampuslauf Innsbruck
description: The Innsbruck Krampuslauf features participants in traditional handcrafted Krampus masks (Larven) and costumes, parading through the city centre on Krampusnacht.

name: Krampuslauf Graz
description: The Graz Krampuslauf features traditional Styrian Krampus figures in early December, drawing large crowds to the city centre.

name: Krampuslauf Munich (Münchner Krampuslauf)
description: Munich hosts Krampuslauf events in early December, reflecting the Alpine tradition shared with neighbouring Austria.

name: Perchtenlauf Salzburg (Rauhnacht)
venue_name: Various villages in the Salzburg region
description: Perchtenlauf parades occur during the Rauhnächte (rough nights) around Epiphany (January 5-6).

name: Schiachperchten and Schönperchten (Pongau, Austria)
venue_name: Alpine villages in Pongau, Salzburg region
description: The Schiachperchten (ugly Perchten) and Schönperchten (beautiful Perchten) parade through Alpine villages during the Rauhnächte. These are among the oldest Alpine winter procession traditions.
